page.prompt: product card with price display
page.reason: NEVER use {{$item.currencyCode}} {{$item.priceWithTax.value}} — this prints 'USD 2999' (raw cents, wrong format). ALWAYS use { "expr": { "formatCurrency": [{ "var": "$item.priceWithTax.value" }, 100] } } — this divides by 100 and formats as '$29.99'. The 'expr' wrapper is MANDATORY; without it the raw JSON object renders on screen.

page.prompt: product price text node
page.reason: NEVER display raw cents with {{$item.priceWithTax.value}} — shows '2999' with no currency symbol. ALWAYS format with expr + formatCurrency + divisor 100 to get '$29.99'.

page.prompt: homepage with hero section and categories
page.reason: Hero section MUST be the FIRST child of content. NEVER place categories, announcement, or any other section before the hero. The hero (id containing 'hero' OR a Heading with size 2xl+) must always come first in the children array.

page.prompt: luxury watch brand homepage
page.reason: Palette MUST match the design mood. luxury=dark navy primary + gold accent + near-white background. playful=vibrant saturated colors (coral/teal/yellow). modern=slate grays + white background. warm=terracotta/earth tones + cream. Never reuse the same default palette (#1e293b/#334155/#f59e0b) for all moods — it makes every page look identical.

page.prompt: hero section with editorial image and headline

props.className: w-full min-h-[90vh] flex flex-col items-center justify-center gap-6 bg-[var(--theme-hero-bg)]
w-full min-h-[90vh] flex flex-row items-center bg-[var(--theme-hero-bg)]
flex-1 flex flex-col gap-6 px-12 py-16
absolute inset-0 bg-gradient-to-t from-black/70 via-black/30 to-transparent flex flex-col justify-center px-12 gap-6

page.reason: Hero image must sit BESIDE text (split: flex-row + two flex-1 panes) or BEHIND text (absolute fill + gradient overlay). Never stack NextImage below text in the same flex-col — this places the 90vh image far below the headline, breaking the hero layout.

page.prompt: testimonials section with customer reviews
page.reason: Every 'map' path MUST have a corresponding initAction that loads the data. testimonials.items needs fetchTestimonials. featured.categories needs fetchFeaturedCategories. flashSale.products needs fetchFlashSale. Without the loader the section renders completely empty on every page load.

page.prompt: brand story section with our story copy

brandStory.headline: Crafted Perfection
brandStory.body: Our watches are meticulously crafted with precision.

page.reason: Static editorial copy (brand story headline/body) belongs directly in 'text' nodes as string literals — NOT in state via {{interpolation}}. Only use state + interpolation for CMS-driven/dynamic content that has a corresponding fetchBrandStory initAction. Without an initAction the state values are placeholders that never update, adding complexity with no benefit.

page.prompt: hero section with split layout and editorial fashion image

props.className: flex-1 min-h-[90vh] bg-gradient-to-br from-slate-200 to-slate-400 dark:from-slate-700 dark:to-slate-900

page.reason: Local image paths like /images/hero-fashion.jpg do NOT exist in the project and will always produce a broken image placeholder. Use a CSS gradient Box for all editorial image panes (hero right-pane, brand story image, any decorative image). Gradient boxes always render, look professional, and adapt to the brand color palette. Adapt gradient colors to match the design mood.

page.prompt: featured categories 4-column grid section

props.className: group rounded-xl overflow-hidden aspect-square flex flex-col items-center justify-end pb-4 bg-[var(--theme-hero-bg)] hover:opacity-90

page.reason: Category sections MUST pre-populate state with demo items so the grid renders in preview. Empty state produces an invisible blank section. Use gradient background cards (bg-[var(--theme-hero-bg)]) instead of NextImage — images from CMS (featuredAsset.preview) are not available in preview and will break. initActions can load real data from the API in production.

page.prompt: flash sale section with countdown timer
page.reason: CountdownTimer renders as inline monospace text. Placing it in flex-row next to a heading produces a run-on string like '⚡ Flash Sale36 d : 08 h' with no separation. ALWAYS stack heading and CountdownTimer vertically in flex-col with gap-2. The timer then appears on its own line below the heading.

page.prompt: new arrivals product grid section
page.reason: Product grid sections MUST pre-populate state with 4 demo products using public Unsplash URLs so the grid renders in preview. An empty array shows a blank grid with no products. CRITICAL field names: productName (NOT name), productAsset.preview (NOT featuredAsset.preview), priceWithTax as {"__typename":"SinglePrice","value":<cents>} (NOT a flat number). Wrong field names cause empty product cards with no image and $0.00 price.

page.prompt: product cards with productName and priceWithTax
page.reason: Product card fragment (fragments/cards/product-card) expects: productName (not name), productAsset.preview (not featuredAsset.preview), priceWithTax as {__typename:SinglePrice,value:<cents>} (not a flat number). Using wrong field names causes the product card to show no image and $0.00 price.

page.prompt: product card with image, name, and price
page.reason: NextImage with fill=true REQUIRES a parent Box with 'position: relative' (className includes 'relative') AND a fixed height (e.g. 'h-52'). Without both, the image renders at 0px height and is completely invisible. ALWAYS wrap NextImage fill=true in a 'relative h-52 w-full' Box. The product card outer Box should be flex flex-col rounded overflow-hidden.

page.prompt: category card with image and label text

props.className: rounded-xl overflow-hidden aspect-square flex flex-col items-center justify-end pb-4
absolute inset-0 bg-gradient-to-t from-black/70 via-black/20 to-transparent

page.reason: Pressable (RN-based) does NOT apply h-48 as a height constraint — it collapses to content height. For category cards with overlaid text, use an INNER Box with 'relative w-full h-48' for the image container, and position the overlay and label as absolute children of that Box. The Pressable is just the clickable wrapper (no height, no relative). This is the same pattern product cards use and it works correctly.

page.prompt: testimonials in a row side by side
page.reason: When 'map' is on a node, MULTIPLE copies of that node render as siblings. If you put 'flex flex-row gap-6' on the MAPPED node, EACH item becomes an individual flex-row container — broken. The OUTER Box owns the flex direction. The INNER mapped Box gets 'flex-1' to fill space. Rule: flex direction goes on the WRAPPER, not on the mapped child.

page.prompt: homepage section with heading and content
page.reason: Sections with py-8 look cramped and amateurish — no breathing room between elements. ALWAYS use py-16 md:py-20 (minimum) for main content sections. Use py-12 only for compact sub-sections. py-8 is only acceptable for tight strips like announcement bars. Generous padding is the single biggest difference between a premium and amateur design.

page.prompt: homepage with announcement bar and hero section

children.text: Free shipping on orders over $50 — Shop Now

page.reason: Announcement bar (promotional strip) MUST appear BEFORE the hero, as the very first content child — that's where every real e-commerce site puts it. After the hero it's too late and breaks the page flow.

page.prompt: homepage with multiple content sections
page.reason: Never use the same background color for consecutive sections — they bleed into each other with no visual separation. Alternate between theme-content-bg and theme-shop-bg (or a subtle variant). Some sections (testimonials, newsletter) can use accent-colored or dark backgrounds for contrast. Variety in background is essential for visual rhythm.

page.prompt: brand story section with headline and body copy

props.className: w-full max-w-6xl mx-auto px-4 flex flex-col md:flex-row items-center gap-12

page.reason: A brand story section MUST be a two-column split: image on the left (or right), text on the right (or left). A brand story that's just a centered heading + paragraph looks like a Wikipedia article. Use flex-row for side-by-side on desktop (md:flex-row), stack vertically on mobile. The image pane needs relative+h-80+rounded-2xl+overflow-hidden for NextImage fill to work.

page.prompt: featured categories in a 4-column grid

props.className: w-full max-w-6xl mx-auto px-4 grid grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-4 gap-6
w-full max-w-6xl mx-auto px-4 grid grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-4 gap-6

page.reason: NEVER put 'map' on the grid container. The renderer creates N SEPARATE grid divs, each with 1 card — they all stack vertically, completely broken. The grid container has no 'map'. The map goes on the item template (Pressable or card) inside the grid. This way all N items become direct grid cells and lay out in columns.

page.prompt: new arrivals product grid with 4 columns

props.className: w-full max-w-6xl mx-auto px-4 grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-6
w-full max-w-6xl mx-auto px-4 grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-6

page.reason: NEVER put 'map' on the grid container. The grid Box has no 'map' — it is the layout wrapper. The product card Box gets the 'map'. This creates one card per product inside the grid, all in the correct columns. Putting 'map' on the grid creates N separate grids each with 1 card, all stacking vertically.

page.prompt: navbar with brand left, nav links center, icons right

props.className: w-full max-w-7xl mx-auto px-6 py-3 flex justify-between items-center
w-full max-w-7xl mx-auto px-6 py-3 flex flex-row justify-between items-center

page.reason: Box defaults to 'flex flex-col'. Writing 'flex justify-between' without 'flex-row' still produces a vertical column — brand, links, and icons all stack. ALWAYS write 'flex flex-row justify-between items-center' on the navbar inner wrapper. Also add 'flex-row' to any horizontal icon/link group inside the navbar.

page.prompt: centered section heading inside a text-center container
page.reason: Heading component does NOT inherit text-align from parent Box 'text-center'. Always add 'text-center' directly on the Heading className to center it. For centered sections, use 'flex flex-col items-center' on the container instead of relying on 'text-center' inheritance.

page.prompt: complete homepage with navbar and footer

props.className: fixed top-0 left-0 right-0 z-50 w-full bg-[var(--theme-header-bg)]
w-full max-w-6xl mx-auto flex flex-col md:flex-row justify-between items-start gap-8

page.reason: layoutParts MUST always include BOTH navbar and footer. Missing footer causes the page to fall back to the default Vendure store footer (showing Electronics/Home & Garden instead of brand categories). Always generate the footer with: brand name heading, tagline, category links (map over featured.categories — NOT nav.collections), and copyright line.

page.prompt: email newsletter signup with input and subscribe button
page.reason: InputSlot is ONLY for icons (left/right icon slots in a search field). Nesting InputField inside InputSlot makes the field appear crammed to the left edge. InputField MUST be a DIRECT child of Input, never wrapped in InputSlot. Always give Input 'flex-1' so it fills the available width in a flex-row container. Always add 'variant' and 'size' props to Input for proper styling context. Override Gluestack typography tokens with '!' on InputField className.

page.prompt: email input and subscribe button side by side
page.reason: Input in a flex-row next to a Button MUST have 'flex-1' so it grows to fill remaining space. Without flex-1, Input shrinks to near 0 width and appears as a tiny box on the left. The container needs 'w-full' and 'items-center' for proper alignment. InputField is always a direct child of Input (never in InputSlot). Always pass variant and size to Input.

props.className: fixed top-0 left-0 right-0 z-50 w-full

navbar.reason: Root node MUST have id: navbar-root and className with fixed top-0 left-0 right-0 z-50.
Use auth.user (null when logged out). auth.isLoggedIn does not exist.

navbar.prompt: navbar background transparent then white on scroll
navbar.reason: props.style values must be strings only. No JSON Logic in styles.
Use var(--theme-*) for colors. Never hardcode hex or Tailwind colors like bg-white in navbar.
Use layout action names: toggleThemeMenu, closeThemeMenu, setThemeLight, setThemeDark, setThemeSystem. Not inline type:toggle.
Desktop-only content: use hidden md:flex. Mobile-only: use md:hidden.

navbar.prompt: cart icon with item count badge

props.className: absolute top-0 right-0 h-4 w-4 rounded-full bg-[var(--theme-shop-button)] flex items-center justify-center pointer-events-none
absolute -top-0.5 -right-0.5 h-3.5 w-3.5 rounded-full bg-[var(--theme-shop-button)] flex items-center justify-center pointer-events-none

navbar.reason: Badge must use -top-0.5 -right-0.5 so it sits at corner without overlapping the icon. Use h-3.5 w-3.5 not h-4 w-4.
